metadata.title: Hallilha — Sharia compliance for freelancers and SMEs
metadata.description: AI-powered Islamic finance platform for solo professionals and teams. Automate Shariah compliance, purification tracking, and zakat reporting in one workspace.
metadata.ogDescription: Real-time Sharia auditing, purification, and zakat dashboards for freelancers and growing businesses.

hero.title: Hallilha… your financial future, on
hero.description: A modern financial workspace for freelancers and SMEs. Automate compliance, ensure purification, and grow wealth with ethical confidence.

about.title: Built for the
about.missionBody: Hallilha was founded on a simple conviction: every Muslim founder deserves access to world-class Islamic financial tools — without the prohibitive cost of traditional Sharia boards. We automate compliance so you can focus on building.
about.point1: Automated Sharia auditing for SMEs and freelancers across the MENA region
about.point2: Real-time purification tracking so you never unknowingly mix funds
about.point3: Partnered with leading Islamic finance scholars and regional banks

features.title: A complete ethical system
features.description: Enterprise-grade tools made simple for freelancers and SMEs to maintain strong Shariah hygiene.

auditor.title: Smart AI Auditor
auditor.description: An AI chat interface that reviews contracts and transactions against approved fatwas in real time — available 24/7.

purification.title: Automatic Purification
purification.description: Automatically identify and isolate non-compliant income sources to track purification with pinpoint accuracy.

contracts.title: Contract Templates
contracts.description: Access a library of pre-audited, ready-to-use Musharakah, Mudarabah, and Murabahah agreements.

zakat.title: Zakat Dashboard
zakat.description: Automatic calculations for hawl and nisab across your holdings and business assets, ready when you pay.

realtime.title: Real-time Monitoring
realtime.description: Optional bank connections for live cash-flow auditing when you need it. Every synced transaction can be scored as it lands.

secure.title: Bank-grade Security
secure.description: 256-bit encryption, SOC 2 compliant infrastructure, and your credentials are never stored on our servers.

starter.title: Starter
features: Documents up to ~50 pages per thread

contact.title: Let's start a
contact.subtitle: Have a question or want to see a demo? Our team typically responds within 24 hours.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us how we can help you…
contact.success: Message sent! We'll be in touch soon.

items.q: Is Hallilha certified by a Sharia Supervisory Board?
items.a: Yes. All compliance rules, purification calculations, and contract templates on Hallilha have been reviewed and approved by our Sharia Supervisory Board, composed of certified Islamic finance scholars.

When Hallilha detects a non-compliant income source (such as interest income from a conventional bank account), it automatically quarantines the flagged amount in a 'Purification Queue'. You can then easily donate it to a charity of your choice directly from the dashboard.

items.q: Can I connect my bank account?
items.a: Yes. Whether you use a personal account as a freelancer or a business account as an SME, Hallilha integrates with major banks in the MENA region via secure open-banking APIs when you choose to connect. We use strong encryption and never store your bank credentials.

The Zakat Dashboard tracks zakatable assets over the lunar year (hawl) and helps estimate your obligation based on current nisab thresholds—for sole traders with simple portfolios as well as larger businesses. It produces a structured report you can review with a qualified accountant or scholar before paying.

Yes. The Free plan includes Halim with 5 AI audit conversations per month (see pricing for document limits). Paid plans start at $9/month for higher quotas. Contact us about Enterprise if you need custom limits or API access.
Hallilha fully supports both Arabic (RTL) and English, with more languages planned. The entire platform — including the AI auditor — works in both languages.

metadata.title: Halim chat — Islamic finance assistant
metadata.description: Chat with Halim for Sharia compliance audits, Islamic finance guidance, and document reviews grounded in AAOIFI-aligned knowledge.
metadata.ogDescription: Your AI workspace for Islamic finance compliance and Sharia auditing.

welcome.title: Halim
welcome.subtitle: Your AI consultant for Islamic finance, Sharia auditing, and compliance advisory.

suggestions.reviewContractSub: Walk through murabaha, ijara, or sukuk-style documentation like a working group: spot riba, gharar, and ownership gaps before sign-off.
suggestions.reviewContractPrompt:
I am reviewing an Islamic finance facility agreement (e.g. murabaha, tawarruq, ijara, diminishing musharaka, or sukuk) for a corporate client.

Please:
1. Summarize the economic substance vs. the legal form and whether it resembles common AAOIFI-aligned structures.
2. Flag clauses that could imply riba, excessive gharar (ambiguity), or invalid sales/ownership.
3. List targeted questions we should raise with the arranger and the bank's Sharia supervisory board.
4. Note any missing operational controls (cron jobs for sales, independent verifications, asset traceability) that auditors usually care about.

Assume this is for professional compliance support—not a personal fatwa—and clearly separate general principles from contract-specific risks.
suggestions.shariaScreeningSub: Due diligence narrative for issuers, ADRs, and mixed revenue: sectors, debt ratios, and non-permissible income — as investment committees expect coverage.
suggestions.shariaScreeningPrompt:
We are evaluating whether to include a listed equity (or fund) in a Sharia-compliant portfolio. The company is in {sector / region} with mixed permissible and questionable revenue lines.

Please:
1. Outline a disciplined screening workflow aligned with mainstream institutional practice (business activity, leverage/interest metrics where applicable, and non-halal revenue purification thresholds).
2. Produce a bullet list of due diligence questions for management, IR, and the fund administrator.
3. Highlight grey areas where policy interpretation differs across providers and where we should escalate to a qualified Sharia committee instead of relying on automated screens.
4. Suggest how to document the decision trail for regulators or clients.

Replace bracketed placeholders with reasonable assumptions if I have not specified them.
suggestions.auditChecklistSub: Control objectives, evidence samples, and recurring findings for internal audit of Islamic windows and full-fledged Islamic banks.
suggestions.auditChecklistPrompt:
Act as a Sharia internal audit co-pilot. We are planning an AAOIFI-oriented review of our Islamic banking retail and corporate products (financing, deposits, treasury, and fee income).

Deliver:
1. A phased audit plan (scoping workshop → data requests → fieldwork → reporting) with clear objectives per product family.
2. A checklist of controls (origination, pricing, collateral, early settlement, promotions) and examples of evidence we should request.
3. Common findings from the market (documentation mismatch, operational shortcuts, misplaced guarantees) and how to word them in an audit observation.
4. A one-page executive summary outline suitable for the board’s Sharia committee.

Keep everything advisory and flag where local regulations may override generic guidance.
suggestions.zakatSub: Trading entities: what enters the zakatable base, ḥawl, school differences, and purification — without pretending a single final ruling.
suggestions.zakatPrompt:
Our company needs a structured briefing on zakat for business wealth for the current lunar year. We hold cash, trade receivables, inventory, prepayments, fixed assets used in operations, and some interest-bearing placements (we already segregate riba income for purification).

Please:
1. Explain which balance-sheet and P&L items are typically included or excluded in trading-entity calculations, and why.
2. Describe how ḥawl (completion of one lunar year) is commonly applied to corporate cash flows—not personal savings.
3. Summarize where major schools differ (trading goods vs. productive assets, treatment of long-term investments) so we know when a qualified Sharia accountant or scholar is mandatory.
4. List documentation the finance team should prepare before any number is published.

Do not output a final cash zakat amount; focus on method, disclosures, and governance.
